data.sentence: Hey Julia! So great to finally have this call — I feel like we've been trying to get this on the calendar for, like, two weeks now.
I know, I know, it's been a crazy January honestly. Like every year I say it's going to slow down after the holidays and then it just... doesn't.

Ha, financial services January — I can only imagine. Julia, I also wanted to introduce you to Ananya Sharma, she's one of our senior engineers on the platform side. I thought it would be good to have her on since you'd mentioned some technical questions around your renewal.
Hi Julia, really glad to be here. I've actually been looking through your account setup before the call, so I'm kind of up to speed on your environment.

Oh perfect, that's great. Hi Ananya! Yeah I had a whole list of things I wanted to talk through so it'll be good to have someone technical on the call too. Aisha, you know me, I always come with a list.

I do know that about you and I love it, honestly it makes my job so much easier. Okay so should we just jump in? I was thinking we'd kind of do a quick check-in on how the platform's been performing for you this year, and then we can talk about the renewal terms and what the next contract period might look like.
Yeah that works for me. So honestly, um, the short version is — we're happy. Like genuinely happy, which is not something I say lightly because our CISO is, let's just say, very particular.
That is so good to hear. Can you tell me a little more about what's been working well? I always want to make sure we understand the specific value drivers for Blackridge.

Sure, yeah. So I think the biggest thing for us has been Aegis Protect. Like our backup and recovery setup — we went through a tabletop exercise in Q4, disaster recovery scenario, and the recovery time estimates we got from the platform were just... they held up really well. Our previous vendor, it was always kind of a guessing game, you know?
That's amazing to hear about the tabletop. Those exercises can really expose gaps and the fact that Protect performed well under that kind of scrutiny is — yeah, that's exactly what we want.

Right, and our team was honestly a little nervous going into it. We had some skeptics internally who were like, is this platform actually going to deliver? And it did, so that was a big win for us in terms of internal credibility.
Internal credibility — that is huge, especially in financial services where you've got so many stakeholders watching security. That's a real win for your team, Julia.

Yeah for sure. And then on the Detect side, um, the threat monitoring has been solid. I mean, we had a few alerts in November that turned out to be nothing, but the visibility we're getting is genuinely better than what we had before. I can actually see what's happening in our environment which sounds like a low bar but, trust me, it wasn't before.
Yeah, the visibility piece is something we hear a lot from customers coming from legacy tooling. It's almost like — you don't know what you don't know until you can actually see it.

Exactly, yeah. And speaking of Detect — I did want to ask, I saw something in... I think it was a forum or maybe a community post, about some kind of processing issue? Like earlier this year? Is that something I should know about?

Yeah, so I want to be completely transparent with you about that. We did have an incident with the event processing pipeline on the Detect side — this was back in January. There was a cascading failure in the ingestion layer that caused a window of reduced visibility for some customers.
Okay, so what happened exactly? Like were Blackridge environments affected?

So I checked your account specifically before this call and the impact to your environment was minimal — you were in a region that recovered faster. But I don't want to sugarcoat it, there was a period of degraded monitoring. The root cause was a single point of failure in the event ingestion layer and we have since deployed redundant processing nodes and implemented a circuit breaker pattern so that one failure doesn't cascade the way it did.

Okay, I appreciate you being straight about it. Honestly that kind of thing happens — I work in security, I know infrastructure is not perfect. What matters to me is how you responded and whether it's fixed.

Absolutely, and we also sent a full post-mortem to affected customers. Julia, I'll make sure you get that documentation if you didn't receive it — it walks through the timeline, root cause, and the remediation steps in detail.
Yes please, send that over. My CISO will want to see it. Actually that's good that you have that — that's the kind of documentation we need for our own audit trail.

Absolutely, I'll get that to you right after this call. Okay so — you mentioned your CISO and audit trail, which actually segues nicely into something I wanted to share. Can I talk about what's coming on the compliance side?
Oh yeah, please. Comply is actually one of the things on my list. We've been using it for the basic reporting but I feel like we're not — we're probably not using it to its full potential.

So this is really exciting — we have a major version coming, Aegis Comply v2, and it's going to be generally available in April. The big thing is on-demand compliance reporting across multiple frameworks simultaneously. So SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, ISO 27001 — you can pull reports on demand rather than waiting for a scheduled run.
Wait, on-demand? Like whenever I want?

Whenever you want, yeah. And the multi-framework support means you're not running separate reports for each framework — it maps your controls across frameworks so you can see your posture holistically.

Oh that is — okay that is huge for us. Because right now when we have an audit coming up I am manually stitching together reports from like three different places and it is not a fun time. Not a fun time at all.

Ha, yeah we hear that a lot. The multi-framework mapping is going to save so much time for teams like yours. And you know, given that you're in financial services, having PCI DSS and SOC 2 in the same view is going to be — I think it's going to be a game changer.
For real. Can I be in a beta or preview or anything? Like I would love to get my hands on that before it goes GA.

Yes! Actually that's exactly the kind of thing I wanted to talk about as part of the renewal conversation. We have early access opportunities for customers who are committed to the next contract period. So that's something we can absolutely build into the terms.
Okay I'm already sold on that. What does the renewal look like? Like what are you thinking in terms of — are we talking same scope, expanded scope?

So I want to ask you first — are there areas where you feel like you need more coverage? You mentioned you're not fully utilizing Comply, and I know you haven't deployed Aegis Identity yet. Is that something that's on your roadmap?

Identity is actually something I've been pushing internally. We have kind of a patchwork IAM situation right now, like different systems that don't talk to each other great. Our IT team has been resistant to changing it but... I think the momentum is shifting. We had a third-party audit in December that flagged it.

The audit flagging it is actually really useful leverage for you internally. And from a technical standpoint, Aegis Identity plays really well with your existing Detect and Protect footprint — the integrations are native so you're not adding complexity, you're actually simplifying your stack.
Yeah that's a good point. The integration story is important for me because I cannot go to my IT team and say this is going to take months to deploy. They will shut it down immediately.

Totally understand. We actually have an implementation playbook specifically for customers who are already on Protect and Detect — the SSO and MFA components can typically be up in a couple of weeks rather than months. Ananya, do you want to speak to that a little bit?

Yeah so for existing customers with your stack, we're looking at a phased rollout where you can do SSO first, get that stabilized, and then layer in MFA policies. Because you already have the identity data flowing through Detect we can shortcut a lot of the discovery work. I'd say realistic timeline is four to six weeks to be fully operational, not months.
Okay. Four to six weeks I can work with. I might need that in writing to show my IT director but — I can work with that.

We can absolutely put together a deployment scope document. That's something I can have our solutions team draft up as part of the renewal package so you have something concrete to bring to your stakeholders.
That would be perfect. Okay so let's say — hypothetically — we do renew and we add Identity. What does the commercial side look like? Like are there multi-module discounts?

So yes, we do have bundling tiers and given that you'd be moving to all four modules that puts you in our highest bundling tier which has meaningful savings compared to individual module pricing. I want to put together a formal proposal rather than throw numbers out right now, but I can tell you it's going to be a very competitive number and I think it's going to make the business case for adding Identity essentially a no-brainer.

I like the sound of that. Okay yeah, please put that proposal together. And include the Comply v2 early access in there — that's actually really important to me, I want to be able to show my CISO something tangible that we're getting ahead of.

Absolutely, that will be in the proposal. I'm going to have it over to you by end of next week — is that timeline okay? I want to make sure we have enough time for you to review before your contract expires.
Yeah end of next week is fine. Our renewal deadline is March 15th so we have a little runway. I just want to make sure we're not scrambling at the last minute.

Totally agree, no last-minute scrambles. I'll have the proposal to you well ahead of that. And Julia — I just want to say, it really means a lot that you guys have had such a positive experience this year. Blackridge is exactly the kind of customer that makes this work meaningful.

Aw, honestly the feeling is mutual. Like I've worked with vendors where you sign the contract and then you never hear from them again and with you guys it's been really different. You're actually responsive, Ananya your team has been helpful when I've had technical questions — it makes a difference.

That means a lot, seriously. Okay, so to recap — I'll send over the post-mortem documentation today, get you the renewal proposal with the all-four-module bundle pricing and Comply v2 early access included by end of next week, and I'll loop in our solutions team on the Identity deployment scope document. Does that cover everything on your list?

Um... yeah, I think that covers it. Oh wait — one more thing. Is there any kind of user training or onboarding for Comply v2 when it goes GA? Because I'm going to be the one rolling it out to my team and I don't want to just hand them a product and say figure it out.

Great question. We'll have full documentation and video walkthroughs at launch, and for early access customers we're planning live onboarding sessions — so you'd actually get that white-glove onboarding experience rather than just docs.
Oh perfect. Yeah that's exactly what I was hoping to hear. Okay I think I'm good then. Aisha, Ananya — this was really helpful, I feel good about where we're headed.

Same here, Julia. Really excited for the next chapter with Blackridge. I'll get that post-mortem doc over to you this afternoon and you'll hear from me by next Friday with the full proposal. Talk soon!
Sounds great. Thanks both of you — have a good rest of your week!
